response.final_response:
# Customer Solution Discovery Brief — Reduce clinician documentation burden

## Problem Summary
Customer needs an AI-assisted discovery and delivery plan for the stated business problem. The first objective is to clarify constraints and define measurable outcomes.

## Clarifying Discovery Questions
- Which workflow step creates the largest burden today? (Find the highest-leverage pilot scope.)
- What compliance or policy controls must any AI output satisfy? (Ensure feasibility and safety before solution design.)
- What baseline metric is available today (time, quality, or throughput)? (Enable measurable before/after verification.)

## Workflow Decomposition
Workflow details to be confirmed.

## Stakeholder Map
- TBD

## Data and Integration Assumptions
- TBD

## Proposed Solution
Build a domain-specific copilot for healthcare that assists the target workflow, surfaces grounded recommendations, and routes uncertain cases to human reviewers.

## Success Metrics
- Cycle time reduction: Primary business value is faster workflow completion. Measurement: Compare median end-to-end task time before and after pilot rollout.
- Quality and safety score: Speed must not degrade outcomes or compliance quality. Measurement: Track QA review pass rate and critical error rate per 100 tasks.
- Adoption by frontline users: Sustained impact requires behavior change, not one-time usage. Measurement: Measure weekly active users and task-assist utilization rate.

## Risks and Mitigations
- Incorrect or low-confidence recommendations in sensitive workflows. Mitigation: Require human review for high-impact actions and confidence-based escalation.
- Poor integration with existing systems causing workflow friction. Mitigation: Start with one bounded pilot workflow and instrument handoff failures.
- Weak change management reduces adoption despite technical quality. Mitigation: Run enablement sessions and collect role-specific feedback each sprint.

## Pilot Plan
Pilot one workflow for 4-6 weeks with a defined user cohort, baseline metrics, weekly risk reviews, and go/no-go criteria tied to metric targets.

## Evaluation Plan
Evaluate discovery quality, measurable value, and risk coverage on each run. Accept changes only when eval evidence improves against baseline.
